channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here are the 7 best Pixel 9 and Pixel Watch 3 features landing in March’s Pixel Feature Drop
item.description: The latest Google Pixel feature drop is here – and it brings scam call protection to the Pixel 9 and Loss of Pulse Detection to the Pixel Watch 3.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Netskope expands SASE footprint, bolsters AI and automation
item.description: Netskope is expanding its global presence by adding multiple regions to its NewEdge carrier-grade infrastructure, which now includes more than 75 locations to ensure processing remains close to end users.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SAP ERP Software: The FOMO Trap and the Rise of Independent Support
item.description: SAP ERP software vendors have shifted their focus from feature-rich upgrades to cloud-based solutions, leaving customers with outdated systems feeling left behind. Discover how Rimini Street offers a viable alternative for SAP customers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sony is now issuing PS Plus compensation following the recent PSN outage
item.description: PlayStation Plus subscribers are officially receiving compensation following the PSN outage last month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD warns its RX 9070 GPUs are strictly ‘UEFI-only’ – and if that sounds worrying, don’t panic, it probably doesn’t affect you
item.description: AMD’s new RDNA 4 graphics cards will only support UEFI, and we’ll explain exactly what this means for your gaming PC.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bitcoin jumps after Trump strategic reserve announcement
item.description: The cryptocurrency market surged over the weekend after President Trump announced his plans to create a strategic crypto reserve and hinted at details about the highly anticipated plan.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Americans split over Musk, federal workforce cuts: Survey
item.description: Americans in a new survey are split over their views of Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), as well as federal workforce cuts during the opening weeks of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The 2025 Terminator? Lab-grown muscle brings biohybrid robot hand to life
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how lab-grown muscles power a biohybrid hand for scalable prosthetics and research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025
item.description: How are agencies refining their AI strategy? The post Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI Readiness: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Discover the essential steps to prepare your organization for artificial intelligence, from human transformation to technical readiness.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World
